stories.title: The Wyvern's Price
stories.text:
Karthax descended through storm-clouds, her wings shredding mist. Once she had hoarded gold in the Ironspine peaks—three centuries' worth, coins stacked like cordywood. Now the metal was *ash*. Worthless. The Radiant Emergence had rewritten matter itself, and all currency had become memory.

She found the merchant caravan in the foothills, thirty souls crossing toward Helmark. They carried spice, cloth, iron tools. *Things with value still.*

"I will guard you," Karthax announced, landing before their lead wagon. The merchants scattered, hands to sword-hilts. "To Helmark's gates. In exchange, you will trade with me what you consider most precious."

The caravan master—a weathered woman named Seela—studied the wyvern with clear eyes. "And if we refuse?"

"Then I hunt alone, as my kind hunted before kingdoms named prices." Karthax's jaw widened in something like a smile. "But you will not refuse. You are afraid of the bandits in the Thornlands. Of the void-touched ruins. I am far more terrible and far more useful."

Seela laughed—a genuine bark. "Then we have a pact."

Three weeks they traveled together. Bandits scattered at Karthax's roar. When a bridge collapsed over the Ironbrook, she plucked wagons across one by one. At a village plagued by hunger-wolves, she drove them into the high peaks, asking nothing but a night's shelter in the square.

At Helmark's gates, Seela opened her personal chest. Inside lay a music-box—brass, intricate, playing a melody no living person had heard before the Emergence. "My grandmother carved this," Seela said. "From old starlight that fell in the wastes. It remembers a song from before the new stars burned."

Karthax took it in one careful claw. The melody spilled out—not gold, not memory, but *longing*. A sound that spoke of hoarding something other than metal.

"This," the wyvern said, "is more valuable than all my lost coins. You have taught me a new hunger." She rose on her hind legs, wings unfurling. "I will remember your caravan, Seela. That is its own currency now."

stories.title: The Ledger-Breaker's Gambit
stories.text: In the shadow-markets of Anthrim, forger Kael discovers that the Hegemon's master-ledger is not merely a record—it is a working. Each false entry they carve destabilizes consensus reality further. When the Counter-House sends word that Sevin the Auditor needs a ledger capable of overwriting the Hegemon's authority in three provinces at once, Kael must decide: forge a document so powerful it could reshape governance itself, knowing that success means the world splinters into incompatible truths, or refuse and watch displaced-folk testimony be erased entirely. The price for such a working is not gold. It is certainty—Kael will forget why they ever believed in truth at all.

stories.title: The Bone-Singer's Debt
stories.text: Kess of the Drowned Hollows receives a visitation from the ante-human dead, who announce that their teaching is being withdrawn. The Primordial Tongue is fading from the world because the cycle is breaking—and if it breaks, the dead will cease to exist in any meaningful form. Kess is offered a bargain: sing the bones of three transmuted merchants into their final shape, completing their ascension and preventing the cycle-break, or watch the dead fade forever. But completing the merchants' transformation may trigger the Spire's full manifestation. Kess must navigate the boundary between honoring the dead and condemning the living.

stories.title: The Wyvern's Hunger Becomes Philosophy
stories.text: Vrekka the Fragment-Hoarder and her war-band find themselves unable to hoard. Wealth has become meaningless—the Radiant Emergence rendered all material value recursive and illusory. In desperation, they begin hunting transmuted merchants, not to eat them, but to consume their consciousness fragments and understand what transcendence feels like. When Vrekka corners Mard the Spice-Merchant at a waystation library, she makes an unexpected offer: join her war-band, become part of the hunt, and together they will devour the secret of meaning itself. Mard must choose between transcendence delayed and damnation assured.

stories.title: The Deepkin Captain's Mirror War
stories.text: Commander Thale of the deepkin encounters her own reflection emerging from the Abyssal Rift—a mirror-Thale who claims to be the original, and that the surface world is the reflection. Both believe they are real. The Tear of the Drowned God, already strained by the Radiant Emergence's recursive nature, cannot hold two versions of the same being. Captain Helos Stormwind, cursed by the Tear itself, becomes the battleground where these geometries war. Thale must negotiate with her own mirror-self to prevent the boundary from collapsing entirely, knowing that only one of them can remain.

stories.title: The Widow's Compass Points Backward
stories.text: In the Bleached Wastes, a widow discovers that the compass carved by her grandmother does not point toward salvation—it points toward the moment before the Radiant Emergence. Following it leads to a pre-human ruin where the air tastes of the eighth world. The widow begins to remember a life she never lived, in a kingdom that no longer exists. When Mardoc the Cartographer's distributed consciousness learns of this, he sends a fragment to investigate. The widow and the fragment must decide whether finding the pre-Emergence world means saving it or destroying it in the process.

stories.title: The Merchant's Fractured Inventory
stories.text: Vex the Merchant's vault—already collapsing from the Radiant Emergence's impossibility—reaches a new threshold: her carefully catalogued stock has begun shifting into forms she cannot recognize. Bolts of silk become living threads. Crates of spice hum with buried consciousness. The inventory itself is transforming faster than she can track it. Vex must navigate her own warehouse as if it were a hostile land, negotiating with artifacts that no longer obey mercantile logic. When a rogue bolt of transformed fabric escapes into Pale Crossing, she must choose between pursuing it or accepting that some goods can never be sold again.

stories.title: The Archive Tower's Ninth Apprentice
stories.text: The Young Arcanist's apprentice arrives at the Spire of Records to study the ninth journal—but finds that the tower itself is hungry. It has begun consuming the apprentice's own memories in exchange for knowledge. Each page of the journal costs another year of life, another relationship, another reason to keep living. The apprentice must choose how much of themselves to spend in understanding whether the Radiant Emergence can be broken. Meanwhile, the tower whispers that the Wanderer is orchestrating these sacrifices on purpose—that every student who reads the journal is a piece of a larger working.

stories.title: The Refugee Scribe's Phantom Testimony
stories.text: Liv the Displaced-Scribe's living archive has begun generating phantom truths—contradictory accounts of the same events, all equally real. Displaced-folk speak of the Emergence happening in three different ways simultaneously. The Hegemon hunters are close, but now they face a new problem: which version of Liv is the real one? The living archive has split into three versions, each claiming to be the true keeper, each with different testimony to preserve. Sevin the Auditor sends word that only one version can survive—the other two will be erased from existence. Liv must decide which version of herself is most worth preserving.

stories.title: The Wanderer's Next Question
stories.text: At a caravansary in the Scorched Reach, the Wanderer finally speaks a statement instead of asking a question: 'The cycle cannot be broken. It can only be frozen.' In the silence that follows, the transmuted merchants stop moving. The Spire stops growing. Reality achieves a momentary equilibrium. But equilibrium is not peace. In the frozen moment, the Wanderer reveals the true cost of the suspension: no one can change anything, ever again. The world becomes beautiful, perfect, and utterly stagnant. A young caravan driver must choose whether to shatter the question and restart the cycle, or accept eternal stability.
